steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to contrast two words that represent the same sound. A valid contrast requires: (1) both words named, (2) both identified as representing the same sound, and (3) the difference between them shown — specifically that they are written and pronounced very differently.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The Thai word "khrok fi" and the Bengali word "bhnosh" are onomatopoeias that represent the sound of snoring.': Names both words and the shared sound, but stops there. It states a similarity, not a contrast — no mention of how differently they are written or pronounced. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Though they both represent the sound of snoring, the onomatopoeias "khrok fi" (Thai) and "bhnosh" (Bengali) are written and pronounced very differently.': "Though" signals contrast. It names both words, establishes the shared sound, and explicitly states they are written and pronounced very differently — hitting all three requirements. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Onomatopoeias, words that imitate the sounds they represent, are often written and pronounced very differently from language to language.': A general statement about onomatopoeias; doesn't name either specific word or pair them for contrast. ✗
  • Choice D — 'The Thai word "khrok fi" which represents the sound of snoring, is an onomatopoeia.': Mentions only one word and makes no contrast at all. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
